component.name: Phone Mirror
component.summary: Wire-free Android screen mirroring and control

description.p: Phone Mirror displays your Android phone's screen on the Linux desktop and lets you control it with your mouse and keyboard, wire-free over your local network. It is built natively for the modern desktop with GTK4 and libadwaita, runs on Wayland, and decodes the video stream with FFmpeg.

ul.li: Live, low-latency mirroring of the phone screen onto the desktop
Mouse, scroll and keyboard control, including UTF-8 text input
The phone's audio played through the desktop's speakers
Automatic device discovery on the local network (mDNS/DNS-SD)
One-time pairing with persisted, reconnectable devices
HiDPI-correct input mapping and a distraction-free, undecorated mirror window

description.p: Initial development release: working UI, device discovery and pairing, and the live mirroring pipeline.
